title: Mybench Assessment Claim
description: One signed assessment claim (MYB-10.1, handoff §3). Strict whitelist like every mybench artifact (additionalProperties:false): a claim carries registry identifiers, commitments, bands, and version identifiers — content fields are structurally impossible (privacy invariant #1). Claims stay local until the THREAT_MODEL §3 revision opens a publish gate (invariant #4, MYB-16.2). The 'no floats' rule cannot be expressed here for the free-form output object; mybench.claims.canonical enforces it on every serialization, and validate_claim runs both checks.

registry_id.description: Descriptor registry entry this claim instantiates (namespace.name). A claim without a registry entry is invalid — the registry (MYB-10.2) is the source of truth for shape, bands, and disclosure.

registry_version.description: Semver of the registry entry the output conforms to; any output-shape change bumps it (handoff §4 rule 6).

scorer.description: The code that computed this claim. Behavior changes bump version (load-bearing, like judge versioning).

measurement.description: null locally; enclave code measurement when attested.

inputs.description: Content-addressed evidence the scorer consumed (handoff §4 rule 5).

corpus_commitment.description: Merkle root(s) over the normalized evidence, lowercase hex.

anchor_refs.description: Optional OTS anchor ids relied upon.

output.description: Descriptor-specific payload; bands, not raw values, where §8 requires. Shape is governed by the registry entry (registry_id@registry_version), not by this schema. No floats — enforced by the canonical serializer.

derivation_class.description: measured = deterministic count/presence/ratio; characterization = taxonomy/archetype assignment, renders at judge-tier styling with confidence (handoff §5).

execution_env.description: Where the scorer ran. Tier presentation for these values is owner-gated (OQ #30) — never mapped to tier names in code.

attestation_evidence.description: MYB-7.18 composite attestation-evidence list; local-unattested claims carry the zero-element case so attested evidence appends later without migration.

signed_at.description: Runner-supplied input timestamp (UTC RFC 3339); scorers never read the clock (handoff §4 rule 2).

signer.description: The verifying key, embedded like anchor batches embed device_pub. kind=dev is clearly non-production: its claims verify only up to the self-run tier.

signature.description: Ed25519 signature (hex) over the canonical compact sorted JSON of this object WITHOUT the signature field.
